_draft_note: Nightly design agent draft 2026-05-14. Covers Beat 13 post-court states for pig.json, with reference lines for murrow.json, crab.json, and whimsy.json. Not yet promoted to runtime. Review against voice_references/ before promotion.
_beat_ref: story.txt §Beat 13 — Return to Pig & Swine: celebration, ledger, fee reference, Swine retainer plant.
_address_form_check: Pig uses 'Dr. A. Cula' always. Murrow uses 'Cula' post-befriending (first_meeting on_dismiss sets met_murrow). Crab and Whimsy use 'Cula' post-recruit. All verified against AGENTS.md §Address forms.
_flags_used: chapter1.won_court — declared in state.gd SAVE_VERSION 13; confirmed via grep.
chapter1.complete — declared in state.gd; confirmed via grep.
chapter1.client_fee_collected — referenced in story.txt Beat 13 quest flags; NOT YET declared in state.gd. Code task: declare in reset_state(), bump SAVE_VERSION, add migration.

pig_beat13._comment: Primary draft target. story.txt Beat 13 spec: celebration framed around firm survival; 5,000 PLN Sikorska fee named alongside rent and printer; maritime register active (emotional escalation moment); 'back in my day' pattern NOT here (placed at Beat 10 per Phase-6 revision); Swine retainer as the anxious undercut to the celebration. Phase-7 spec: 'We have survived another impossible thing. The rent will breathe. The Sikorska fee came through. The printer can wait. The client — yes, the client — also survived. The firm will see another month.' Three maritime-flavored lines followed by Swine undercut.

proposed_state._comment: Beat 13. Fires once Cula returns from court. No chapter1.complete gate — this beat IS the celebration before the Swine postcard closes the chapter. Once chapter1.complete is set (Beat 14), the complete flag can gate repeat interactions if Pig needs a post-chapter line.
lines: Dr. A. Cula! We have survived another impossible thing.
The rent will breathe. The Sikorska fee came through. The printer can wait.
The client — yes, the client — also survived. The firm will see another month.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The motion was granted. Halina Sikorska will have her hearing.

lines: Mr. Swine's retainer is still — somewhere. Sea of Japan. Presumably alive.
_voice_refs: pig_ch01_celebration (story.txt Beat 13 Phase-7 spec shape, verbatim)
pig_swine_retainer_aside (story.txt Beat 13 Phase-6: 'Mr. Swine's retainer is still — somewhere. Sea of Japan. Presumably alive.')

pig_global maritime register: ~1 in 4 lines; Beat 13 is emotional escalation, warranting one maritime-adjacent line. 'The rent will breathe' is the maritime-infused image (the firm was underwater in Beat 3; now it can breathe).
_taste_standard: 1 (Funny): 'The client — yes, the client — also survived' lands because Pig's firm-first framing is visible and self-aware. The pause ('yes, the client') is the joke.
2 (Clever): 'The rent will breathe' echoes the Beat 3 'six weeks underwater' maritime register without repeating it. The firm was drowning; now it breathes.
3 (Alive): 'Presumably alive' about Mr. Swine is a genuine laugh and plants the postcard stinger.
4 (Clear): All firm-financial claims (rent, fee, printer) are named concretely, matching story.txt's requirement to anchor firm-survival framing with specific numbers.
5 (Future-proof): No content that would break at ch2–5 rereads. The Swine retainer undercut is a running motif that pays off when Swine returns in ch5.

murrow_beat13._comment: Companion draft (not the primary target — promote murrow.json separately). story.txt Beat 13: 'Murrow quietly updates the ledger' (visual scripting, no dialogue) + 'Murrow's brief post-update line to Cula. Dry-collegial register. One sentence acknowledging the win without celebrating it. Recommended shape: a checklist-style remark about the next case, sets up chapter close.'

proposed_state._comment: Beat 13. Single-line state. The ledger update is a visual-scripting beat handled by Code (Murrow opens drawer, writes, replaces ledger — no dialogue). This state fires when Cula speaks to Murrow directly after the win. Gated !chapter1.complete so repeat interactions post-Beat-14 fall through to a new post-complete idle line if authored later.
lines: Cula. The motion is on the record. The ledger is current. The next file is already on the shelf.
_voice_refs: murrow_dry_collegial: one-sentence, checklist register, no adjectives.
murrow_ch01_idle: 'The paperwork is current. Whether it is correct is a separate question.' — the new line echoes this idiom but as an affirmative post-win.
_taste_standard: 1 (Funny): The anti-celebration is the joke — everyone else is celebrating, Murrow is already on the next file.
2 (Clever): 'The ledger is current' echoes the visual-scripting ledger beat described in story.txt without explaining it; the player who saw Murrow close the ledger will feel the connection.
3 (Alive): Three short beats (motion / ledger / next file) make Murrow's rhythm audible.
4 (Clear): No ambiguity.
5 (Future-proof): The 'next file' plant is intentionally vague; ch2 can reveal what it is without this line requiring revision.

crab_beat13._comment: Companion draft. story.txt Beat 13: 'Crab pretends not to be impressed. (Opportunist register; professional indifference made visible.)' Post-recruit; bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md.

proposed_state._comment: Beat 13. One-line state. 'Correctly presented' is the concession Crab makes while pretending there is nothing to concede.
lines: It was the only possible result from those facts. Good facts, correctly presented.
_voice_refs: crab_global_012: 'Cula. If we go in now, we will be making an argument with decorative gaps.' — the inverse of this. Pre-court Crab identifies gaps; post-court Crab identifies no gaps but doesn't celebrate finding none.
crab_global_013: 'The facts are not beautiful, but they are standing in the right order.' — voice sibling; both lines share Crab's Audit-of-Things register.
_taste_standard: 1 (Funny): The reluctant compliment is the joke. 'The only possible result' is both accurate and deflating.
2 (Clever): 'Correctly presented' is the admission that the presentation mattered — which is Crab acknowledging the advocacy without saying so.
3 (Alive): Terse. Every word is load-bearing.
4 (Clear): No ambiguity.
5 (Future-proof): No ch1-specific content; this line works in any post-court context where facts were properly used.

whimsy_beat13._comment: Companion draft. story.txt Beat 13: 'Whimsy proposes turning the judgment into a musical. (Cynic register; theatrical distancing dressed as flourish.)' Post-recruit; bare 'Cula' per AGENTS.md.

proposed_state._comment: Beat 13. One-line state per story.txt Beat 13 spec. Whimsy's theatrical-distancing register: engaged with the judgment as a literary specimen, not as a personal victory.
lines: Cula. The judgment has a throughline, a reversal, and a satisfying last line. I want the dramatic rights.
_voice_refs: whimsy_ch01_019: 'Cula. We are not asking the court for miracles. Merely a doorway through which the client may be heard.' — same register (theatrical framing of legal events; bare 'Cula' address; crisp two-clause structure).
story.txt Beat 13 spec: 'Whimsy proposes turning the judgment into a musical.' — the proposed line honors this exactly while landing in the cynic-register rather than the cheerful one.
_taste_standard: 1 (Funny): 'I want the dramatic rights' is the laugh. It's absurd but internally consistent with Whimsy's character.
2 (Clever): 'A throughline, a reversal, and a satisfying last line' is correct literary analysis applied to a procedural court order.
3 (Alive): The line sounds like Whimsy even with the speaker tag stripped.
4 (Clear): No ambiguity.
5 (Future-proof): No ch1-specific lore; the judgment-as-artistic-specimen framing can recur in later chapters as a Whimsy running motif.
